title: fix: websim use gen.pollinations.ai with claude-large
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Websim gets a brain transplant
gist.blurb: Websim just got a major upgrade. It now defaults to Claude-large and features a new landing page where you can pick your preferred AI model before spinning up a simulation.
gist.summary: The Websim app has been updated to use the gen.pollinations.ai endpoint, defaulting to claude-large, and now features a landing page with a model selector.
gist.impact: You can now choose your preferred model—including Claude, Gemini, GLM, and Kimi—right from the new starting interface.
gist.visual_concept: Websim is about generating web interfaces, and this PR adds a model selector and switches the default to Claude. The visual should feature Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot interacting with a retro computer that displays a dropdown menu or a selection of glowing 'brains' or gems representing different models.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a cute pixel art bee mascot (yellow body, dark brown stripes, pink blush, small blue wings) and a chunky monitor-head robot looking at a retro computer screen displaying a dropdown menu of different glowing gems. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
